channel.title: Weekly Weather Reports on mel-land
channel.description: Recent content in Weekly Weather Reports on mel-land

item.title: Week 4
item.description: Fourth week of my weekly weather reports series.

item.title: Week 3
item.description: I guess I didn’t take any photos of clouds since the last post? Kinda funky.

item.title: Week 2
item.description: I liked the tree and stuff. I honestly have no recollection of where this is? Maybe near frat row? This was near the hospital. OH I realize that the previous photo was also here, just a different direction! :) Wilshire and the urban hellscape that is Los Angeles has some modicum of beauty among its development, for once. /rolls-eyes I thought this was a nice color so I zoomed in!

item.title: Week 1
item.description: I took this photo when walking with home with my good friend Matt. :) I think that was the last time I will see him at UCLA as he’s going to Caltech now for his teaching/post-doc job. I took the the remaining photos while walking from my friend Jacob’s performance of “The Waitress”! It was amazing. One of my absolute favorite plays that I’ve seen. :) He did a fantastic job!

item.title: May 2023
item.description: Westwood Boulevard might have blocked some of the sky but what remained was pretty darn nice. :) This reminds me of my high school fish-bowl sky photo! On the corner of Gayley and Lindbrook. Those monolithic, empheral, white mountains above the construction site brings some much needed beauty lol. These IFOs (Identified Flying Objects) are quite convincing! (They have been identified to be the lights in my friend’s apartment.)

item.title: Pre-College
item.description: Here are my favorite clouds from my high school years. I took this photo before my zero-period P.E. class in my sophomore year of high school. It was my favorite photo that I’d ever taken at that time. :) God’s white udders fill the sky with its blue milk. I can’t identify this color because I’m incredibly colorblind but it still looks really cool. It looked like I was in a fish bowl when I took this photo.
